condition: Severe Pneumonia or Very Severe Disease
action: Give first dose of appropriate antibiotic. Refer urgently to hospital.
explanation: Any general danger sign or chest indrawing or stridor in a calm child indicates severe pneumonia or very severe disease.

action: Give oral amoxicillin for 5 days. Advise mother when to return immediately. Follow up in 2 days.
explanation: Fast breathing without danger signs indicates pneumonia needing oral antibiotics.

action: If coughing > 14 days, refer for TB assessment. Soothe throat and relieve cough with a safe remedy. Advise mother when to return immediately. Follow up in 5 days if not improving.
explanation: Cough without fast breathing or danger signs is usually a simple cold.

action: Give fluid for severe dehydration (Plan C). Refer urgently to hospital if cholera is suspected or no IV access.
explanation: Two or more danger signs of dehydration mean severe dehydration.

action: Give fluid, zinc supplements, and food for some dehydration (Plan B). Advise mother when to return immediately. Follow up in 5 days if not improving.
explanation: Two or more signs of some dehydration mean the child needs ORS and monitoring.

action: Give first dose of appropriate antimalarial and antibiotic. Treat to prevent low blood sugar. Give one dose of paracetamol for high fever (>=38.5°C). Refer urgently to hospital.
explanation: Any general danger sign or stiff neck in a child with fever requires urgent referral.

action: Give recommended first-line oral antimalarial. Give one dose of paracetamol for high fever. Advise mother when to return immediately. Follow up in 3 days if fever persists.
explanation: Fever with a positive malaria test (or in a high-risk area) without danger signs.
